stories.title: Trump says Iran 'doing a very poor job' in reopening the Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The fragile ceasefire agreement was tested again on Friday after Iran refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Hezbollah traded strikes in Lebanon, and Kuwait was attacked with drones.

stories.title: Gaza families still unable to bury dead six months into ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: About 10,000 Palestinians remain missing, believed to be buried under rubble of collapsed buildings during Israel's war.

stories.title: How Pakistan emerged as a mediator in the Iran conflict – video explainer
stories.summary: Mediating a deal at the 11th hour between Iran and the US, Pakistan has emerged as an unexpected negotiator between the two countries and is now preparing to host their delegations for peace talks. stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: India cracks down on social media jokes criticizing prime minister
stories.summary: As criticism of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's response to the Iran war grows, government censors are scrambling to knock down memes that use the Indian leader as the punchline.

stories.title: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Beirut – video
stories.summary:
Footage shows bulldozers and diggers clearing rubble from central Beirut as Israeli strikes continued in Lebanon. More than 300 people were killed after the announcement of a ceasefire in the Iran war on Tuesday night.

stories.title: Six months into ‘ceasefire’, Gaza suffers under persistent Israeli attacks
stories.summary: Gaza's devastation grows as over 72,000 people killed and 172,000 injured amid Israeli violations of US-brokered truce.

stories.title: Nations tried to impose a digital fog of war in Iran. The results are mixed
stories.summary: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

stories.title: War in Iran strands tons of tea in Kenyan port
stories.summary: Iran war-linked shipping disruptions have stranded 8 million kg of tea in the Kenyan port of Mombasa, costing the industry about $8 million a week in mounting losses.

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon to hold ceasefire talks
stories.summary: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to hold direct ceasefire talks for the first time in decades in a bid to halt the violence in southern Lebanon.
